message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  SARS-CoV-2, the causative agent of COVID-19, is responsible for over 24 million infections and 800,000 deaths since its emergence in December 2019. There are few therapeutic options and no approved vaccines. Here we examine the properties of highly potent human monoclonal antibodies (hu-mAbs) in a mouse adapted model of SARS-CoV-2 infection (SARS-CoV-2 MA).
                  <jats:italic>In vitro</jats:italic>
                  antibody neutralization potency did not uniformly correlate with
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  activity, and some hu-mAbs were more potent in combination
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  . Analysis of antibody Fc regions revealed that binding to activating Fc receptors is essential for optimal protection against SARS-CoV-2 MA. The data indicate that hu-mAb protective activity is dependent on intact effector function and that
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  testing is required to establish optimal hu-mAb combinations for COVID-19 prevention.
                </jats:p>
title: Antibody potency, effector function and combinations in protection from SARS-CoV-2 infection in vivo
